sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal (kindred to חָשָׁה, σίζω, σιγάω). [Not given as a verb in Thes. except as formed from הַס which stands as an interjection.]

Piel, imper. apoc. הַס be silent! silence! an onomatopoetic expression for commanding silence; like the Germ. ft! pft! from which have been formed the roots הָסָה, הָשָׁה; Hab 2:20 Zep 1:7 Zec. 2:17 Jud. 3:19 Amo 6:10. Adv. silently, Amo 8:3. LXX. σιωπήν. Plur. הַסּוּ Neh 8:11.

Hiphil, to command to be silent, to still (a people), Num 13:30.
